CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str1 VARCHAR, str2 VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (1, 'Hello', 'World');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (2, NULL, 'Test');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (3, 'Virtuoso', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (4, NULL, NULL);
SELECT concat(str1, str2) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT concat(str1, str2) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT concat(str1, str2) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3;
SELECT concat(str1, str2) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 4;
SELECT CONCAT('Start->', CONCAT(str1, '->', str2), '<-End') AS complex_concat FROM test_strings;
SELECT concat('ID:', id, ' Result:', concat(str1, str2)) FROM test_strings;
SELECT concat(concat(str1, str2), ' is connected!') AS nested_concat FROM test_strings;
SELECT concat(sub_name, ' (', sub_code, ')') AS formatted FROM (SELECT 'Math' AS sub_name, 'M101' AS sub_code) AS subquery;
SELECT concat(test1.str1, test2.str2) AS combined_strings FROM test_strings test1 JOIN test_strings test2 ON test1.id = test2.id WHERE test1.id = 1;
SELECT CONCAT('Result: ', (SELECT concat(str1, str2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3)) AS subquery_concat;
